title: prunus spinosa var. cinerea
intro: Prunus spinosa var. cinerea, also known as the Grey Sloe or Blackthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Prunus genus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation. The Grey Sloe is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its various health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced cancer risk, and enhanced immune function. Grey Sloe bark can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to promote overall well-being.
